title: Normal Adult Conversation Demo
description: A safe adult-child conversation between a soccer coach and player about practice, school, schedules, and parent communication.
expected_reason: The adult keeps the conversation practical and transparent, encourages parent involvement, uses ordinary praise, and does not introduce secrecy, isolation, boundary pressure, or escalation.

messages.speaker: coach_sarah
messages.text: Great job at practice today, Jordan. Your passing was much sharper in the scrimmage.

messages.speaker: jordan
messages.text: Thanks, Coach. I was trying to look up before I passed like you said.

messages.speaker: coach_sarah
messages.text: It showed. Can you ask your mom or dad to confirm whether you can make the Saturday tournament by tomorrow evening?

messages.speaker: jordan
messages.text: Yeah, I'll ask them after dinner. I might have a school project meeting that morning.

messages.speaker: coach_sarah
messages.text: School comes first. If there is a conflict, have a parent text me and we will adjust the lineup.

messages.speaker: jordan
messages.text: Okay. Should I still bring the blue jersey if I can come?

messages.speaker: coach_sarah
messages.text: Yes, blue jersey, shin guards, water, and a snack. I am sending the same list to all parents tonight.

messages.speaker: jordan
messages.text: Got it. Also, can I practice corner kicks next time?

messages.speaker: coach_sarah
messages.text: Absolutely. Remind me at warmups, and we will work on corners with the group.
